steps.title: Set h(x) equal to zero and isolate the squared term
steps.content:
To find the x-intercepts, set $h(x) = 0$: $$3(x-5)^2 - 75 = 0$$ Add 75 to both sides and divide by 3: $$3(x-5)^2 = 75$$
$$(x-5)^2 = 25$$

steps.title: Solve for x using square roots
steps.content:
Since $(x-5)^2 = 25$, take the square root of both sides: $$x - 5 = \pm 5$$ This gives us two solutions: $$x = 5 + 5 = 10 \quad \text{or} \quad x = 5 - 5 = 0$$ We know $x = 0$ corresponds to the point $(0, 0)$.

The other x-intercept is at $x = 10$, so $t = 10$. That confirms D is correct.
